in this type of content for our purposes of, you know, driving traffic back to the site or having us show up in answer engines or does that kind of voice, does that matter?

So that's a stylistic choice or like a tone choice rather than one that impacts SEO or AI optimization.
What's more important is the readability of the content.
So we want to make sure that the content is easy to read in the sense that it has like bullet points, not all the time.
We want to make sure that the H2s are structured in a very direct way, that each section is distinct from the other section so that we're using tracking to kind of isolate.

Qualify it with, you know, additional descriptors if you want to kind of represent the whole group of consulting expertise.
So for BES content like this, you'll tend to see rimkus engineers, architects, and other technical specialists or something of that nature to kind of encompass the broad range of expertise.

For example, to say building consultant sounds pretty innocuous, but is actually a specific role that someone could have.
They could be, you know, be qualified as a building consultant.

Again, that's not to say engineers is wrong.

Yeah, yeah.
So internal linking is a part of our article generation workflow.
It's the next to last step in the process.
So it uses your sitemap, and it crawls it, and then it identifies appropriate URLs to drop into the content.
And I would say it gets it right about 70% or 80% of the time.
We do have to remember them sometimes, especially...
Especially if it's, like, a blog post that isn't as relevant to link to a specific anchor text.
And then we will also, like, as I'm editing, I might notice, like, places where an internal link would be useful.
So I'll leave a note for the content manager and be like, okay, can you take a look and see if Rimkus has something on their site that, like, would be a good fit to link here.
We've also had clients who, internally, they have just developed, like, a list of 20 or 30 URLs that they want to prioritize with internal links.
Because internal links are important for SEO.
If you send a lot of, like, internal links towards one page, then Google understands that it's important.
So if you do have something like that internally or if you want to develop it, you can send it over and then we'll add that.
Like, as we're editing, we're just going to pick the good URLs to include.

Yeah, so blogs are typically around 1,500 words long up to, for most of our clients, or up to like 2,000 words.

The, the, what's important is to cover a topic comprehensively, and make sure that we're providing all the information someone will be interested in a blog post, which does mean that the blog posts are on the longer side.
What we find is that people will skim blogs, and it's more important to actually make the content easy to skim by using H2s and H3s, so they, they can quickly find the information they need.
Rather than if we,
Provide a shorter blog, it's maybe quicker to read, but we might not provide all the information that they're looking for.
